reasoning: The question asks for a 30-day window in France with more than ten times the baseline of (1 + the 30-day fatality average over the last 360 days). As of May 2025, the baseline requires about 17.5+ ACLED-recorded fatalities in a single 30-day period. Because ACLED filters out non-political/non-conflict violence, France has only seen 9 tracked fatalities over the past year. Hitting 18+ fatalities in 30 days almost strictly requires a major mass-casualty terrorist attack (similar to the 2015 Paris attacks or 2016 Nice attack), or a completely unprecedented lethal riot. The baseline base rate for such an event in any given month in France is historically around 1.5% to 2%. There are no ongoing mass-fatality events in France as of May 25, 2025, heavily suppressing the probability for the June 1 immediate cutoff. For further dates, the probability slightly increases reflecting standard time-horizon uncertainty, but remains extremely low due to the rare "black swan" nature of a 10x fatality spike. New Caledonia's unrest fatalities (e.g. May 2024 riots) are not counted under "France" by ACLED standards, as mathematically confirmed by the 1.750 reference value. I assign my probabilities near the 0.05 rule minimum, climbing slightly to reflect greater uncertainty for later dates.
